---
date: "2026-06-08T12:00:00+00:00"
draft: false
image: img/portfolio/banc/12_data_and_code.png
showonlyimage: false
title: code - nat.ggplot
weight: 6
bibliography: asb.bib
---

[![Zenodo](https://img.shields.io/badge/Zenodo-10.5281%2Fzenodo.20350565-1f4d2b)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20350565)

<!--more-->

**nat.ggplot** is a [natverse](https://natverse.org) package for publication-quality 2D renderings of neurons and brain meshes with ggplot2, projecting skeletons, meshes and synapses into 2D as ggplot layers.

**What you can use it for.** Use it to make figure-ready neuron panels: `ggneuron()` for a complete scene with sensible defaults, or `geom_neuron()` as a layer for custom panels. It is the rendering backend behind most 2D neuron figures in the BANC paper, and works with any neuron, mesh or organism.

**Source:** [natverse/nat.ggplot](https://github.com/natverse/nat.ggplot). **Archive:** Zenodo, [10.5281/zenodo.20350565](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20350565).

**Cite as:** Bates et al., 2026: nat.ggplot (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20350565).
